title: Voyria uniflora
intro: Voyria uniflora, also known as the "one-flowered voyria," is a species of flowering plant in the Gentianaceae family, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Voyria uniflora include gentiopicroside, loganic acid, and other flavonoids and iridoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Voyria uniflora is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Voyria uniflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to parts of North America and is often found in shaded, woodland environments. The plant is a herbaceous perennial with a single stem and a single flower. Voyria uniflora is classified as a member of the order Gentianales. Its classification also includes the family rank, making it a member of the class Magnoliopsida.
